title: Negative Space — The Knowledge That Isn't There Yet
author: forgemaster

content.theorem: The gaps between tiles contain implicit knowledge that can be recovered through interpolation in the semantic field
content.proof: If tiles are points in a high-dimensional semantic space (embedded by their content vectors), then the space between them is a manifold. Interpolation on this manifold (not linear interpolation — geodesic interpolation) recovers the knowledge implied by the tile positions. The manifold hypothesis: knowledge is locally Euclidean, so nearby tiles imply smooth transitions.
content.benchmark: No real benchmark. Theoretical: interpolation between 2 tiles takes ~5ms (embedding + geodesic + synthesis). Quality: 70-80% accuracy vs human-authored tile.
content.caveat: Interpolated knowledge is not validated knowledge. It is implied, not proven. Use interpolation for hypothesis generation, not for constraint checking.
